title: Aral-fn
description: Function representation for ephemaral verification. Describes a pure data transformation: a function that takes a typed input (plus optional parameters) and returns the same type with some fields changed via spread-and-override. This is the proof boundary — everything before this format is unproved (parsers, LLMs), everything after is proved correct in Lean 4.

inputType.description: The type being transformed. Matched case-insensitively against the invariant root prefix (e.g., 'Record' matches 'record.value >= 0').

inputFields.description: All fields on the input type relevant to verification. These become 'in-*' and 'out-*' constants in the SMT encoding. Fields not listed here are assumed unchanged by spread.

params.description: Extra parameters beyond the input object. These are free variables in the verification — Z3 will try all possible values.

name.description: Parameter name (must also appear in params).

type.description: Type name, matched case-insensitively against .aral root prefixes to apply invariants as preconditions.

typedParams.description: Typed parameters. When present, the pipeline can route .aral files by type: invariants whose root matches a typed param's type are applied as preconditions on that parameter.

assigns.description: Which fields change and to what expression. Fields not listed here are preserved by the spread operator.

optionalFields.description: Fields that may be absent (nullable/optional). For each field listed here, the pipeline generates a presence flag ('has-{field}') constrained to 0 or 1. Invariants referencing optional fields are implicitly guarded by presence.

fieldName.description: Which output field is being set.

Expr.description: An expression in the function body. Seven variants: numeric literals, field/parameter references, arithmetic (+, -, *, /), if-then-else, rounding, and collection sum.

lit.description: Numeric literal. Compiled to SMT-LIB Real. Convention: 1 = true, 0 = false for boolean fields.

field.description: Reference to a field. Simple refs (name only) resolve against inputFields or params. Qualified refs (qualifier + name) are fields on a typed parameter (e.g., config.rate).

qualifier.description: Optional qualifier — the parameter name for qualified field access (e.g., 'config' for config.rate). Omit for simple input-type fields.

op.description: Arithmetic operator: add (+), sub (-), mul (*), div (/ total: a/0=0).

ite.description: If-then-else: evaluates cond, returns then-branch if true, else-branch if false.

round.description: Rounding: applies a rounding mode to an expression.

mode.description: Rounding mode: floor (toward -∞), ceil (toward +∞), half_up (standard rounding).

sum.description: Sum over collection items. Collection is a plain field name (always on the input type). Body is a per-item expression — field refs inside are item-scoped.

collection.description: Collection field name on the input type (e.g., 'entries').

body.description: Per-item expression. Field refs are item-scoped (e.g., 'amount', 'quantity').

BoolExpr.description: A boolean expression used as a condition in if-then-else. Supports comparisons, boolean logic, negation, and field presence checks.

op.description: Comparison operator: eq (==), neq (!=), gt (>), lt (<), gte (>=), lte (<=).

isPresent.description: Field presence check for nullable/optional fields. Returns true if the field has a value. Uses the same field reference format as Expr.field.

name.description: Field name to check presence of.

qualifier.description: Optional qualifier for qualified field access (e.g., typed parameter fields).
